Best Software

Best Construction Client Portal Software (2026)

A good client portal eliminates the phone tag, email chains, and "what's the status?" texts that eat your admin time. Here are the platforms with the best client-facing features in 2026.

✓ Updated April 2026

Your clients want three things: to know what's happening, to feel heard when they have questions, and to make decisions without friction. A client portal delivers all three — when it's done right. The worst portals are portals in name only: a login page that shows a static PDF and a contact email. The best portals give clients real-time visibility into schedule, budget, photos, and documents, plus a simple way to approve changes, sign contracts, and ask questions — all in one place.

Client portal quality is increasingly a differentiator for contractors. Clients who work with portal-equipped companies feel more confident, generate fewer support calls, and leave better reviews. For high-end residential work especially, the portal experience is part of the service.

Quick comparison

Software Portal Strength Client Can Do Best For Starting Price
Buildertrend ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ View schedule, approve COs, message, pay invoices Residential GCs $499/mo
Houzz Pro ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Approve proposals, make selections, view timeline Design-build remodelers $99/mo
JobTread ⭐⭐⭐⭐ View budget, approve COs, pay online Small-mid GCs $99/mo
Procore ⭐⭐⭐½ Owner access to documents, RFIs, reports Commercial/enterprise Custom
CoConstruct ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Selections, message threads, daily logs Custom home builders Merged into Buildertrend

#1 Buildertrend — Best overall client portal for residential

Buildertrend's client portal is one of the most complete in the industry. Clients get their own login (no extra cost) and can see their project schedule with real-time updates, view daily logs with photos from the site, approve or reject change orders with digital signatures, view and pay invoices online, and message the project team directly without using email.

The portal is white-labeled and professional — it looks like your company's software, not Buildertrend's. Clients can access it from any browser or from the dedicated iOS/Android app. For residential GCs managing homeowners through multi-month projects, this level of transparency significantly reduces "where are we?" communication.

Buildertrend — The most complete client portal for residential and light commercial GCs.

Start Free Trial →

#2 Houzz Pro — Best client portal for design-build and remodelers

Houzz Pro's client experience is arguably the most polished in the industry for design-build and remodeling work. The client portal isn't just a document dump — it's built around the design and selection process that defines high-end residential remodeling.

Clients can review and approve proposals with digital signatures, make finish and fixture selections from contractor-curated option boards (with budget impact shown in real time), view the project timeline, and communicate through message threads tied to specific topics rather than a generic inbox. The Houzz marketplace integration means clients can browse and select products directly, with quotes feeding into the project budget automatically.

For contractors competing for high-value residential clients, Houzz Pro's client experience is a genuine competitive advantage. Clients who've used the portal consistently cite it as a reason they'd refer the contractor to friends.

Houzz Pro — Client portal designed for the design-build experience. Selections, approvals, and messaging in one place.

Try Houzz Pro →

#3 JobTread — Best value client portal for growing GCs

JobTread's client portal is clean, functional, and priced accessibly. Clients can log in to view the current project budget (with the level of detail you choose to share), approve change orders with electronic signatures, review and pay invoices online, and access uploaded documents like contracts and warranty information.

The portal is simpler than Buildertrend's but covers the essentials well. For GCs who want client transparency and electronic approvals without paying premium platform prices, JobTread delivers strong value.

JobTread — Client portal with budget transparency and electronic approvals at an accessible price.

Try JobTread Free →

#4 Procore — Best for commercial owner access

Procore's owner access module is designed for commercial real estate developers and public agency owners who need project oversight — not just client communication. Owners with Procore access can view project documentation, track RFI and submittal logs, review budget reports, and monitor schedule progress in real time without requiring the GC to generate manual reports.

This is different from a residential client portal. It's designed for sophisticated owners who know how to read a schedule or a budget variance report, and who want direct access rather than curated updates. For commercial GCs working with owner-operators who demand transparency, Procore's owner access is the gold standard.

What makes a great client portal?

Real-time schedule visibility

Clients should be able to see where their project stands without asking. A live schedule view — updated by the project team, visible to the client — answers "when will you start the kitchen?" before it becomes a call or text.

Photo documentation

Regular photo updates show progress in a way text can't. The best portals organize photos by date and phase, creating a visual history of the project that clients genuinely enjoy looking at. It also protects contractors by documenting work before it's covered by drywall.

Change order approvals

Electronic change order approval is table stakes. Clients should be able to review, approve, or reject change orders in the portal with a digital signature. No printing, scanning, or emailing PDFs.

Messaging and communication logs

All project communication should flow through the portal's messaging system, not personal email or texts. This creates a searchable record and keeps project discussions in context.

Online payment

Clients should be able to pay invoices directly from the portal via ACH or credit card. This eliminates the check-by-mail cycle and gets you paid faster.

Document access

Contracts, warranties, lien waivers, permits, and other project documents should be accessible to clients from the portal — not stored on the contractor's computer and emailed when requested.

Client portal adoption tips

  • Set it up before the project kicks off, not after problems arise. Clients who start using the portal early adopt it as their default communication channel.
  • Walk clients through it during the kickoff meeting. A 10-minute demo removes the "I don't know how to use it" barrier.
  • Redirect questions to the portal. When clients text or email questions that could be answered in the portal, gently redirect: "I've uploaded the schedule to your portal — you can check status there anytime."
  • Update it regularly. A portal that hasn't been updated in two weeks loses client trust. Daily log photos and weekly schedule updates are the minimum.

Bottom line

For residential GCs who want the most complete client portal, Buildertrend leads the category. For design-build and remodeling firms competing for premium clients, Houzz Pro offers the most polished client experience in the industry. For growing GCs who want client transparency at an accessible price, JobTread delivers solid value. And for commercial GCs working with sophisticated owners, Procore's owner access module is in a class of its own.
